findings.description: Unsafe __import__ call in find_python_module allows arbitrary module import via YAML tags
findings.reasoning: The find_python_module method calls __import__(name) with user-controlled 'name' parameter when unsafe=True. This occurs when parsing YAML with !!python/module tags using unsafe constructors (UnsafeConstructor, FullConstructor). An attacker can craft malicious YAML to import arbitrary modules, potentially leading to code execution through module initialization code.

evidence.dataflow_analysis: The unsafe parameter is set to True by UnsafeConstructor.find_python_module (line 717) and is used when loading YAML with unsafe loaders. User-controlled YAML input containing !!python/module tags flows directly into the name parameter.
evidence.notes: This vulnerability affects PyYAML library's unsafe loading capabilities. When users load YAML from untrusted sources with unsafe loaders, arbitrary module imports can be triggered.

exploit_payload.code_snippet:
import yaml

# Malicious YAML that imports os module through !!python/module tag
malicious_yaml = '''
!!python/module "os"
'''

# Using unsafe loader (default Loader inherits from UnsafeConstructor)
yaml.load(malicious_yaml, Loader=yaml.Loader)

# This will trigger __import__('os') and potentially execute code in os module initialization
# An attacker could target modules with dangerous __init__ code
exploit_payload.scenario: Attacker supplies malicious YAML with !!python/module tag to trigger arbitrary module import
exploit_payload.prerequisites: Requires use of unsafe YAML loader (UnsafeConstructor, FullConstructor, or default Loader)
exploit_payload.category: Arbitrary module import leading to potential code execution
